name: Coinbase Commerce API
what_it_does: Provides a REST API and hosted checkout for merchants to accept crypto payments (BTC, ETH, USDC, and others) non-custodially, with funds going directly to the merchant's wallet.
use_cases: Create payment charges programmatically and redirect customers to a hosted Coinbase Commerce checkout page
Detect confirmed crypto payments via webhooks and automatically fulfill digital orders or unlock content
Build a custom crypto checkout UI using the charges API to generate payment addresses without using hosted pages
Accept USDC stablecoin payments to avoid crypto volatility while still enabling permissionless settlement
Integrate crypto payment options alongside fiat (Stripe) for global customers who prefer on-chain payments
not_for: Custodial payment processing where you need to hold, convert, or refund funds programmatically — Commerce is non-custodial and does not support refunds via API
High-frequency microtransactions where per-transaction blockchain confirmation times and fees are prohibitive
best_when: Best when you want to accept crypto payments with minimal infrastructure, zero monthly cost, and funds settling directly to your own wallet without a custodial intermediary.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need automated refunds, fiat conversion, or settlement speed guarantees — blockchain confirmation times and non-custodial design make these impossible without additional layers.

auth.notes: Single API key passed in the X-CC-Api-Key header. Webhook payloads include an HMAC-SHA256 signature in X-CC-Webhook-Signature header that must be verified to prevent spoofed payment events.

pricing.paid_tiers: 1% transaction fee on all payments; no monthly subscription cost
pricing.notes: No monthly fee makes it accessible for low-volume merchants. 1% fee is competitive with traditional payment processors for crypto. Distinct from Coinbase Advanced Trade API (trading) and Coinbase Prime (institutional).

performance.rate_limits: 100 requests per second per API key; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST errors return standard HTTP status codes with JSON body containing error type and message. Webhook error scenarios (underpayment, expired charge) are well-documented with distinct event types.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Charge creation supports an idempotency key header (Idempotency-Key) to prevent duplicate charges on network retry. Not all endpoints support idempotency keys.
known_agent_gotchas: Charges expire after a configurable window (default 60 minutes) — agents must handle CHARGE:EXPIRED webhook events and create new charges rather than retrying expired ones
Webhook signature verification is critical: spoofed CHARGE:CONFIRMED events without verification would trigger false fulfillment in automated workflows
Underpayments (user sends less than the charge amount) generate a separate CHARGE:UNRESOLVED event — agents must decide whether to accept partial payment or request top-up
Blockchain confirmation times vary widely by network and congestion — agents should treat CHARGE:PENDING as unconfirmed and only fulfill on CHARGE:CONFIRMED

security_score_components.security_notes: Webhook HMAC verification is essential and well-documented. API key has full account access with no scope restrictions — a compromised key can create and manage charges. Non-custodial design limits blast radius of API compromise.
